data.title: Invitation to press and analyst conference in Stockholm
data.description:
AB Volvo

The Volvo Group report for the first quarter will be published on April 23, 2025 at 7:20 a.m. CEST.

A press conference will be held at Tändstickspalatset, Västra Trädgårdsgatan 15 in Stockholm at 9:00 a.m. CEST. The Volvo Group will be represented by President and CEO Martin Lundstedt and CFO Mats Backman.

Link to webcast: https://qreport.volvogroup.com/

Press and analysts who want to participate in the press conference via conference call, may call the telephone exchange at +46 10 444 18 30 from 8:00 a.m. CEST.

Please be aware that press and analysts who want to attend the press conference in person at Tändstickspalatset needs to pre-register via investorrelations@volvo.com.

April 9, 2025

Journalists wanting further information, please contact:Claes Eliasson, Head of Media Relations

+46 76 553 7229

press@volvo.com 

For more information, please visit volvogroup.com

For frequent updates, follow us on LinkedIn

The Volvo Group drives prosperity through transport and infrastructure solutions, offering trucks, buses, construction equipment, power solutions for marine and industrial applications, financing and services that increase our customers’ uptime and productivity. Founded in 1927, the Volvo Group is committed to shaping the future landscape of sustainable transport and infrastructure solutions. The Volvo Group is headquartered in Gothenburg, Sweden, employs more than 100,000 people and serves customers in almost 190 markets. In 2023, net sales amounted to SEK 553 billion (EUR 48 billion). Volvo shares are listed on Nasdaq Stockholm.

https://news.cision.com/ab-volvo/r/invitation-to-press-and-analyst-conference-in-stockholm%2Cc4133046

Press release - Invitation to press and analyst conference in Stockholm

Mack M1 P 1860x1050

data.title: PageGroup Shares in Red as Weak Hiring Confidence Hits First-Quarter Profit
data.description:
PageGroup shares declined nearly 3% Wednesday midmorning after the British recruiter reported a year-over-year drop in first-quarter gross profit, as subdued client and candidate confidence weighed on hiring activity.

Gross profit for the three months ended March 31 fell to 194.2 million pounds from 220 million pounds a year earlier, as clients continued to tighten recruitment budgets. The cautious approach led to a slowdown in the hiring process, with fewer offers extended and a lower rate of interviews converting to accepted positions.

Both permanent and temporary recruitment segments contracted during the quarter, though temporary hiring outperformed as employers opted for more flexible arrangements. Regionally, the UK recorded the sharpest contraction, with gross profit down 12.7% in constant currencies. Europe, the Middle East, and Africa followed with a 12% decline, while Asia Pacific dropped 11.1%.

"The conversion of interviews to accepted offers remained the most significant challenge, as ongoing macro-economic uncertainty continued to impact confidence, which extended time-to-hire. Despite the decline in gross profit, activity levels remained robust," said Chief Executive Officer Nicholas Kirk.

To streamline operations, PageGroup plans to simplify its management structure and boost the efficiency of its business support functions. The initiatives are expected to result in a one-off cost of 15 million pounds, partially offset by savings of 5 million pounds in 2025 to eventually deliver annualized savings of 15 million pounds per annum.

Looking ahead, the company said it remained cautious about its 2025 outlook, citing an "increasingly unpredictable economic environment" following the implementation of new tariffs.

data.title: VW's Traton posts 10% drop in Q1 sales on challenging market environment
data.description:
Volkswagen's truck unit Traton reported a 10% drop in first-quarter deliveries on Wednesday, citing challenging market environment.

The company said it sold 73,100 trucks and buses in the first quarter, compared with 81,100 a year earlier.

"The U.S. market is continuing to suffer from a low transportation activity," the company said in a statement, adding market uncertainties caused by that possible impact of tariffs had a negative effect on first-quarter deliveries.

data.title: Dampskibsselskabet NORDEN A/S - weekly report on share buy-back
data.description:
NORDEN

ANNOUNCEMENT NO. 78 – 9 APRIL 2025

On 7 February 2025, NORDEN initiated a share buy-back programme in accordance with Regulation No. 596/2014 of the European Parliament and Council of 16 April 2014 (MAR) and Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1052, (Safe Harbour regulation). The share buy-back programme runs from 7 February 2025 up to and including no later than 24 April 2025. For details, please see announcement no. 20 of 6 February 2025.

Under the share buy-back programme, NORDEN will purchase shares for up to a total of USD 18 million (approximately DKK 128 million). The following transactions have been made under the programme:

Date

Number of shares

Average purchase price (DKK)

Transaction value (DKK)

Total, last announcement

654,000

186.92

122,243,870

07/04/2025

18,000

154.18

2,775,240

08/04/2025

18,180

163.93

2,980,247

Accumulated

690,180

185.46

127,999,357

Since the share buy-back programme was initiated on 7 February 2025, the total number of repurchased shares is 690,180 at a total amount of DKK 127,999,357.

With the transactions stated above, NORDEN holds a total of 2,719,971 treasury shares, corresponding to 8.50%. The total number of shares in NORDEN is 32,000,000. Adjusted for treasury shares, the number of shares is 29,280,029.

During the same period (7/4-25 - 8/4-25) major shareholder, Motortramp A/S, has sold 10,958 shares. Please see announcement no. 22/2025 and daily reporting.

This concludes the share buy-back.

Kind regards,

Dampskibsselskabet NORDEN A/S

Klaus Nyborg

Chairman

 

For further information:

Therese Möllevinge, Head of Investor Relations, tel.: +45 41 37 16 38, e-mail: thm@norden.com

data.title: European Gas Prices Slump as Trade War EscalatesMarket Talk
data.description: European natural-gas prices continue to trade lower in early exchanges, with the benchmark Dutch TTF contract dropping 4.5% at 34.59 euros a megawatt hour as a widening global trade war threatens to hit energy demand. President Trump's tariffs on global trade partners took effect on Wednesday, including a 104% levy on China that sets the scene for a dramatic escalation between the world's two largest economies. Reciprocal tariffs between Washington and Beijing are prompting China's LNG buyers to resell U.S. cargoes. Meanwhile, EU members are reportedly negotiating changes to gas storage targets that could soften this year's goals amid broader concerns over low inventory levels. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: SEN. MARIA CANTWELL (D-WA) IS INTERVIEWED ON MSNBC`S ALL IN
data.description:
TRANSCRIPT

April 08, 2025

NEWS EVENT

SEN. MARIA CANTWELL (D-WA)

SEN. MARIA CANTWELL (D-WA) IS INTERVIEWED ON MSNBC`S ALL IN

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

20 East Thomas Road, Suite 2200

Phoenix, AZ 85012

asc.info@viqsolutions.com

Copyright 2025. Provided under license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

All materials herein are protected by United States copyright law

and/or license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc., and may not be

reproduced, distributed, transmitted, displayed, published or

broadcast without the prior written permission of

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

You may not alter or remove any trademark, copyright or other

notice from copies of the content.

SEN. MARIA CANTWELL (D-WA) IS INTERVIEWED ON MSNBC`S ALL IN

APRIL 8, 2025

SPEAKERS:

SEN. MARIA CANTWELL (D-WA)

CHRIS HAYES, MSNBC HOST

CHRIS HAYES, MSNBC HOST: And joining me now is Senator Maria Cantwell. She`s Democrat of Washington. She`s a member of the Senate Finance Committee.

Senator, it`s good to have you on. First, will you just explain what this legislation would do?

SEN. MARIA CANTWELL (D-WA): Well, we`re saying that this issue that is constitutionally given to Congress is an issue that Congress needs to reassert itself. The fact that a president can throw an economy not just ours but a global economy into such havoc without really consulting or talking to Congress who really were the agent of our constituents, and do all of that without any kind of input is just wrong.

So, we`re saying that Congress should pass this legislation, reassert our control over this trade policy and require the president to get approval for these actions.

HAYES: The — when this bill came out, again, co-sponsored with Chuck Grassley, has seven Republicans on it, which is, you know, that is impressive math given where the country is, right? That`s like — that`s a shockingly high degree of bipartisan consensus given how the math has been working normally.

The White House immediately issued a veto threat, that the bill would severely constrain the tariff powers, it would eliminate Trump`s ability to have leverage over foreign countries, ergo you would need to override that veto. The other avenue as I understand it is they are essentially abusing a 1970 statute not designed for them to do this in which the President declares an emergency and pursuant to that emergency power is given this leverage to unilaterally set these tariffs.

And my understanding is there are efforts of foot in both houses to essentially push a resolution that would revoke that emergency declaration. Do I have that right?

CANTWELL: Yes, there`s a lot of effort now because as you see 401(k) programs have been impacted in the Stock Market and there`s a lot of Americans that don`t have pensions anymore and have 401(k) plans. You see the businesses like the retailers and consumer electronics organizations and apparel companies all coming and saying please, please do something about this. We are still in a period of inflation, and making things more expensive for the consumer is a really, really bad idea.

So, our colleagues now are joining discussions about how to reassert our role in this. So, the same legislation that Senator Grassley and I introduced was introduced in the House of Representatives with a number of Democrat and Republican co-sponsors, and this is after the president threatened his veto. So, I think what my colleagues who are listening to their constituents are going to do are going to look for the legislative path to respond to their constituents and say, yes, I`m going to try to hold down these costs on you.

HAYES: One of the things I`ve watched develop, and I`m curious to hear if you feel this way, is watching this process of denial play out, because I think a lot of folks on Wall Street, a lot of Republicans, a lot of Republican officeholders thought this is all a bluff to create leverage, what he really wants to do is do a bunch of deals. But my understanding of listening to the President is that he doesn`t want the deals. He wants the tariffs. The tariffs are the point. If he does the deal, and the tariffs go away, he doesn`t get the income which he can then put towards his big tax cuts for the rich, and he doesn`t get this like neo mercantilist vision of the glory years of McKinley. And I wonder if your colleagues are coming to understand that like he is serious about this.

CANTWELL: Well, I think you did a good rendition in your opening of his commitment over a campaign trail to this. And I think while people probably thought that sounded good on a campaign trail, even though people may not have understood exactly what the tariff meant which you`re also showing in your segment, that now all of a sudden it`s real in that plummeting of the market, in those small businesses, Main Street alliance and others saying, oh my gosh I`m going to end up paying this because I`m a small business, I`m not going to be able to get out of this. And now they`re calling their members of Congress saying, you have to do something to fix this.

And our colleagues now know as you saw from the Senate Finance Committee today as we questioned the Trade Ambassador that the plan is not clear. The outcome that the President seeks is not clear. And so, I do think that they caught people off guard. The size and the scope and, you know, a lot`s been made of different countries. Why would you even you know try to retaliate on them? It`s all been now seeping into our colleagues.

And I think it`s a really bad place to be. You know, you don`t want to be — you have anybody between you and your constituents. You want to listen to what they say. And they`re getting a very — they`re giving a very clear signal right now, they`re very concerned in an inflationary time, that this is just going to raise costs. And they are not even sure what the end result strategy means.

And you talked about the manufacturing. The Biden Administration did grow 700,000 manufacturing jobs over what we lost during COVID. But that`s on next-generation innovation. That`s on next-generation energy. That`s on, you know, bringing the semiconductor supply chain back. It`s about innovation and being the leader in the next set of manufacturing jobs, not trying to somehow think you`re going to tariff semiconductors back into the United States.

HAYES: Yes. And we should note that they`re blocking some of the federal expenditures for some of that investment in clean tech in hopes of like bringing back a lot of sock factories as far as I can tell.

Senator Maria Cantwell, thank you very much.

CANTWELL: Thank you.

END

data.title: Rykadan Unit's JV Sells Stake in Decorative Materials Distributor
data.description:
Quarella Holdings, a 50-50 joint venture owned by Rykadan Capital subsidiary Noble Stone Investments and Lead Rise International, will sell the sale share and sale loan of Quarella Group to Quarella Global for HK$240 million, according to a Tuesday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Quarella Group distributes construction and interior decorative materials, the filing said.

The consideration comprises HK$1 for the sale share and HK$240 million for the sale loan, the filing said.

The sale will help Quarella Holdings repay certain loans to Rykadan Capital and allow Rykadan to streamline its asset portfolio, the filing said.

Noble Stone is 87% owned by Rykadan.

data.description:
RNS Number : 3115E Franchise Brands PLC 09 April 2025  

9 April 2025

FRANCHISE BRANDS PLC

("Franchise Brands", the "Group" or the "Company")

Directors' dealings

Franchise Brands plc (AIM: FRAN), an international multi-brand franchise business, announces that on 8 April 2025 two directors of the Company, Stephen Hemsley (Executive Chairman) and Nigel Wray (Non-Executive Director) each bought 15,000 Ordinary Shares in the Company, at a price of 137.5 pence per share.

Following these trades, their interests in the Ordinary Shares of the Company are as follows:

Director/PDMR

Number of Ordinary Shares in the Company

Percentage of the issued share capital

Stephen Hemsley

22,850,000

11.79%

Nigel Wray

16,021,858

8.27%

The table at the end of this announcement provides further information in accordance with the requirements of the UK Market Abuse Regulation.

Enquiries:

Franchise Brands plc

+ 44 (0) 1625 813231

Stephen Hemsley, Executive Chairman

Peter Molloy, CEO

Andrew Mallows, CFO

Julia Choudhury, Corporate Development Director

Stifel Nicolaus Europe Limited (Nominated Adviser and Joint Broker)

+44 (0) 20 7710 7600

Matthew Blawat

Nick Harland

Allenby Capital Limited (Joint Broker)

+44 (0) 20 3328 5656

Jeremy Porter / Daniel Dearden-Williams (Corporate Finance)

Amrit Nahal / Joscelin Pinnington (Sales & Corporate Broking)

Dowgate Capital Limited (Joint Broker)

+44 (0) 20 3903 7715

James Serjeant (Corporate Broking)

Malar Velaigam / Colin Climie (Sales)

MHP Group (Financial PR)

+44 (0) 20 3128 8100

Katie Hunt / Hugo Harris

+44 (0) 7884 494112

franchisebrands@mhpgroup.com

About Franchise Brands plc

Franchise Brands is an international, multi-brand franchisor focused on B2B van-based service with seven franchise brands and a presence in 10 countries across the UK, North America and Europe. The Group is focused on building market-leading businesses primarily via a franchise model and has a combined network of over 600 franchisees.

The Company owns several market-leading brands with long trading histories, including Pirtek in Europe, Filta, Metro Rod and Metro Plumb, all of which benefit from the Group's central support services, particularly technology, marketing, and finance. At the heart of Franchise Brands' business-building strategy is helping its franchisees grow their businesses: "as they grow, we grow".

Franchise Brands employs almost 650 people across the Group and there are over 3,000 people in the franchise community.

For further information, visit www.franchisebrands.co.uk

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Wednesday at 5 AM ET
data.description:
Global Markets Fall, Bond Yields Spike as Trump's Tariffs Take Effect

Stock markets dropped again, paired with a big jump in bond yields, as President Trump's sweeping tariffs came into effect.

U.S.-China Brawl Takes Center Stage in Global Trade War

A striking escalation of trade hostilities shows the two economies are digging in for a protracted fight.

India's Central Bank Cuts Key Rate, Signals More Support for Economy

India's central bank delivered a back-to-back rate cut and signaled more monetary support ahead as U.S. trade tariffs raised alarm about slowing global growth.

New Zealand Central Bank Cuts Rates, Keeps Door Open to More

The Reserve Bank of New Zealand lowered interest rates on Wednesday, making it one of the first central banks to respond to the growing economic threats posed by the escalating trade war.

Tariffs Threaten Layoffs in French Industry, Minister Warns

U.S. trade tariffs could lead to job losses in the worst-affected French industries, the government minister said, with cosmetics, luxury goods and aeronautics among the most exposed.

South Korea Announces Emergency Support for Auto Sector Against U.S. Tariffs

South Korea's government has announced emergency support measures for the country's auto sector to help ease the impact of U.S. President Trump's tariffs.

Trump Considers More Farm Aid. It Might Not Be Enough.

Any aid package might largely benefit larger farms and leave many smaller players in the lurch as happened during the 2018 trade war.

Fed Minutes Are Coming. Here's What Investors Should Look For.

Investors are betting on interest rate cuts. Minutes of the March FOMC meeting will show where Federal Reserve officials stood on easing.

Trump's Tariffs Put Fed Chair Powell in a 'No-Win Situation'

A trade war ties the Fed's hands by pushing up inflation at the same time that uncertainty is sapping growth.

The Dip Buyers Braving the Market During Stocks Carnage

The tariff selloff tests the long-held practice, with Wall Street warning about the blow to growth.
